title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran hostilities, ignited by US-Israeli airstrikes on February 28, 2026, have prompted massive US military deployments—including the 82nd Airborne Division, thousands of Marines, and special operations forces—to the Middle East, fueling trader consensus at near-100% odds for US forces entering Iranian territory by April 30. Recent combat search-and-rescue missions by special forces, confirming physical ground entry for downed pilots near Isfahan, represent the first verified terrestrial operations, though disputed under market rules excluding involuntary entries. Pentagon preparations for potential raids on Kharg Island or nuclear sites persist amid Trump's April 8 deadline for Strait of Hormuz access, with Iran threatening retaliation against any ground invasion. Escalation risks remain high ahead of intensified strikes.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2026 FIFA World Cup at 16% implied probability, closely trailed by France (14%), England (11%), Argentina (9%), and Brazil (9%), as qualification wrapped March 31 with all 48 teams set post-playoffs that introduced dark horses like Jordan, Curaçao, and Cape Verde, deepening the field. Groups drawn last December separate top seeds—Spain, France, England, Argentina—until semifinals, amplifying path uncertainties amid strong recent form: Spain's Euro 2024 pedigree and Yamal-Pedri emergence, Mbappé-led France's friendly win over Brazil, England's depth despite Foden concerns, Messi's Argentina experience, and Brazil's Rodrygo ACL absence. No fresh injuries in the last 48 hours sustain the tight, competitive dynamics heading into June kickoff.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom leads Polymarket trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.3% implied probability, propelled by his recent high-profile media blitz—including a February New Yorker profile, Guardian podcast, and memoir promotion—positioning him as a sharp Trump critic with executive experience and national fundraising networks amid post-2024 party soul-searching. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.3%, buoyed by progressive grassroots appeal and speculation over a presidential or Senate bid, while Sen. Jon Ossoff's 6.6% reflects buzz from his viral February anti-Trump Senate speech elevating his moderate Southerner profile. Former VP Kamala Harris sits at 4.3% as she weighs options post her California governor demurral. In this fragmented field, 2026 midterm results, donor shifts, and polling trends could consolidate support behind a frontrunner by signaling electability in swing states.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran rejected a Pakistani-brokered 45-day ceasefire proposal on April 6, demanding a permanent end to hostilities before reopening the Strait of Hormuz, as mediators from Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey push for an immediate truce followed by 15-20 days of negotiations. President Trump deemed the plan insufficient in a press conference, threatening to decimate Iran's bridges and power plants by Tuesday midnight if no agreement materializes, amid ongoing US airstrikes on over 13,000 targets and a successful rescue of a downed US pilot. This sixth-week conflict, ignited by US-Israel strikes on February 28, heightens escalation risks, with backchannel diplomacy offering slim prospects for de-escalation absent major concessions.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus for the 2028 presidential winner at 17.8%, edging California Governor Gavin Newsom at 16.8%, driven by recent Republican straw polls affirming his frontrunner status—including CPAC's March results showing Vance at 53% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 35% for the GOP nomination—bolstered by his incumbency edge post-2024 victory. Newsom's proximity reflects Democratic fragmentation and his rising national profile via book tours and family deliberations on a potential run. This closely contested field, with Rubio third at 10.3%, highlights early-cycle uncertainty, where 2026 midterms could separate contenders through battleground results, party control shifts, Trump endorsements, or policy achievements in foreign affairs and domestic agenda.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President J.D. Vance holds strong trader consensus at 37.6% as the natural heir apparent to President Trump, who faces term limits barring a 2028 run, bolstered by his incumbency and alignment with MAGA priorities. HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. leads at 49%, driven by his high-profile role implementing Make America Healthy Again reforms, endorsement of Trump, and populist appeal resonating in prediction markets despite his independent background. Marco Rubio's 21.8% odds surged recently after reports emerged that Trump privately polled major donors on Rubio versus Vance, with most favoring the Secretary of State. Ahead of 2026 midterms, these early dynamics reflect donor influence, cabinet visibility, and base enthusiasm shaping the open primary field.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder command 39.5% implied probability as 2026 NBA champion favorites, anchored by their dominant 62-16 record—the league's best—clinching the West No. 1 seed and full home-court advantage through playoffs, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander shattering scoring consistency records amid minimal injury concerns. San Antonio Spurs trail at 16.4% on a 59-19 mark, propelled by Victor Wembanyama's elite two-way dominance despite a recent narrow loss at Denver. Boston Celtics hold 11.9% as defending champs with 53-25 and East No. 2 seeding behind surging Detroit Pistons (57-22, 5.5%), but traders price in East depth and playoff experience gaps favoring West powers like Nuggets (50-28, 8.5%). Late-season clinchings and clean health slates reinforce this consensus.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' George Russell holds the trader consensus edge at 43.5% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, ahead of teammate Kimi Antonelli at 31.1%, reflecting the silver arrows' early-season dominance with a 45-point constructors' lead after three Grands Prix. Antonelli surged to the top with 72 points via victory in China—his maiden F1 win in a 1-2—and a second triumph at Suzuka, capping back-to-back poles, while Russell opened with an Australian GP win before a Japanese battery failure and safety car timing dropped him, costing the lead by nine points. Team principal Toto Wolff greenlit an intra-team title fight sans contact, but traders favor Russell's experience over the precocious rookie's consistency in the long haul, with Ferrari's Charles Leclerc trailing at 49 points.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's elite ball-striking and two prior Green Jacket wins (2022, 2024) anchor trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability for the 2026 Masters, bolstered by his victory at The American Express to open the season and no finish outside the top 25 since 2024. Jon Rahm (7.8%) and Bryson DeChambeau (7.5%) trail closely, fueled by flawless top-five LIV Golf finishes for Rahm—including a Hong Kong win—and DeChambeau's back-to-back LIV triumphs, signaling sharp major form at Augusta National. Defending champion Rory McIlroy (6.5%), fresh off completing his career Grand Slam last year, arrives unburdened but faces repeat pressure amid a deep 91-player field. Favorable dry, breezy weather and Collin Morikawa's lingering back concerns beyond the top tier keep the race competitively tight, with historical Augusta unpredictability amplifying underdog paths.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 7?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Finland as the clear frontrunner at 37.5% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026, driven by Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin," which dominated Finland's UMK national final in late February with triple the points of rivals, blending violin-driven classical flair, rock edge, and dance beats for jury and televote appeal. The duo leads bookmakers and OGAE fan polls, bolstered by a strong first semi-final slot revealed last week. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem") and France's Monroe ("Regarde!") trail at around 10%, offering jury-friendly ballads with France's direct final qualification edge, while Greece's Akylas ("Ferto") and Australia's Delta Goodrem ("Eclipse") hold fan momentum. Rehearsals loom as the key swing factor ahead of semis on May 12 and 14.

title: Elon Musk # tweets March 31 - April 7, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 31 12:00 PM ET to April 7, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Elon Musk posting 280-299 times from March 31 to April 7, with 60.5% implied probability, driven by the real-time tally of 275 posts through April 6—averaging 39 daily—per XTracker data synced early April 7. This pace aligns with his sustained high-volume cadence seen in prior weekly markets, like the March 27-April 3 resolution at 260-279, but traders anticipate a subdued final day amid weekend patterns and no major catalysts sparking a tweet storm. The 260-279 range at 25.5% reflects caution on even lighter April 7 output, while 300-319 (9.8%) hedges against a late surge from viral political or xAI developments. With hours left in the period, any escalation in public feuds or announcements could shift odds rapidly, underscoring the crowd wisdom in these high-engagement celebrity posting markets.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) to secure third place in the Colombia Chamber of Representatives election by total curules won, reflecting the National Electoral Council's (CNE) completion of 100% escrutinio by late March 2026, which solidified preliminary preconteo trends from the March 8 vote. Under proportional representation across departmental circumscriptions, the PLC amassed a strong national vote share of around 11%, translating to a firm third-most seats behind Centro Democrático (CD) and Partido Conservador Colombiano, amid a fragmented Congress with no majority. Low turnout and traditional party resilience bolstered this positioning. Realistic challenges would require successful legal disputes, recounts in pivotal departments, or CNE revisions altering D'Hondt allocations before final certification.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Péter Magyar at 65.5% implied probability to become Hungary's next prime minister following the April 12, 2026, parliamentary election, reflecting recent polls showing his centre-right Tisza party leading Viktor Orbán's incumbent Fidesz by wide margins—such as Medián's March survey with Tisza at 58% versus Fidesz at 35% among decided voters, and Europe Elects' average of 49% to 41%. This surge stems from Magyar's rapid rise as a former Orbán ally turned opposition leader, capitalizing on public discontent amid economic pressures and EU tensions. Orbán retains 34% odds due to Fidesz's incumbency advantages, gerrymandered districts, and recent by-election wins, though fears of electoral interference and mutual accusations of foreign meddling, including Russian involvement, heighten uncertainty ahead of the vote.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 98.2% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting the Committee's March 18 decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.5%-3.75% amid solid economic data. March nonfarm payrolls rose 178,000—reversing February's decline—with unemployment steady at 4.3%, while February CPI held at 2.4% annually, supporting the Fed's view of contained inflation despite oil price spikes from the Iran conflict. The dot plot projects one cut later in 2026, aligning trader consensus on stability. Upcoming March CPI on April 10 could challenge this if significantly hotter, potentially boosting hike odds, or softer data might nudge cut probabilities higher.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating Middle East tensions, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz and Iran-related risks, have driven WTI crude oil futures sharply higher to around $113 per barrel in early April 2026, with intraday spikes exceeding $116 amid heightened supply disruption fears. Offsetting this rally, OPEC+ agreed to unwind voluntary production cuts, adding up to 1.65 million barrels per day starting this month and an additional 206,000 bpd in recent quotas, while EIA data showed U.S. crude inventories surging 5.5 million barrels to a near three-year high of 461.6 million for the week ended March 27. Traders eye weekly EIA petroleum status reports, potential OPEC+ adjustments, and geopolitical developments for near-term volatility, alongside global demand trends from China and macroeconomic risk sentiment shaping April's price range.

title: Counter-Strike: MIBR vs EYEBALLERS (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 4 match between MIBR and EYEBALLERS in the PGL Bucharest Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 7 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MIBR" if MIBR win the match against EYEBALLERS.

This market will resolve to "EYEBALLERS" if EYEBALLERS win the match against MIBR.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.description:

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the decisive Swiss round 4 high matchup at PGL Bucharest 2026 group stage, MIBR faces EYEBALLERS with the winner advancing to playoffs in the $1.25M event. MIBR rebounded strongly from an opening 0-2 loss to Astralis, grinding out 2-1 wins over BC.Game and Legacy behind standout performances from insani (high 1.20+ rating) and kl1m, entering with a 2-1 record. EYEBALLERS, ranked #37 to MIBR's #34, notched upsets via FaZe's forfeit and a 2-0 over NRG but dropped 0-2 to The MongolZ, relying on veteran rifler JW and AWPer Ro1f amid recent roster stability. No head-to-head history; MIBR holds edges in handicap stats (53% 2-0 wins past three months) and key maps like Anubis (88% win rate), shaping trader consensus toward Brazilian resilience in this BO3 decider.

title: Iran military action against ___ by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country on the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will Iran strike Saudi Arabia in March?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country between March 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET(ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict that erupted on February 28, 2026, with massive US and Israeli airstrikes targeting Iranian missile sites, air defenses, and infrastructure, Iran retaliated through drone and ballistic missile barrages on Israeli cities like Petah Tikva and Bnei Brak on March 31, alongside strikes on a US airbase in the region. Iranian officials have signaled readiness for at least six months of war, including potential escalation against UAE bases aiding US operations, as reported in late March. Recent US and Israeli strikes have degraded Tehran's medium-range missile capabilities, per April 3 assessments, amid President Trump's threats of intensified action absent a ceasefire, leaving diplomatic negotiations and further retaliations as key wildcards.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the Iran-Israel/US conflict persisting beyond mid-2026, with December 31 implied at around 85% probability, reflecting sustained military escalations and stalled diplomacy over the past five weeks since U.S.-Israeli airstrikes began on February 28 targeting Iran's nuclear sites, ballistic missiles, and leadership. Iranian missile barrages continued into Israel, downing two U.S. aircraft on April 3—the first such losses—while U.S. special forces conducted daring rescues deep in Iran and joint strikes hit petrochemical and infrastructure targets. Tehran rejected recent U.S. ceasefire proposals, signaling no de-escalation, amid reports of top Iranian officials killed. Upcoming diplomatic signals or regime shifts could alter odds, but current air superiority and regime destabilization efforts sustain trader expectations of a prolonged campaign.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Real Madrid CF win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Real Madrid CF wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Bayern Munich a narrow edge in this Champions League quarterfinal first leg at the Bernabéu, fueled by Harry Kane's recovery from an ankle injury and expected start up top after completing full training sessions. Real Madrid benefits from home crowd energy and returns for Vinícius Júnior alongside Federico Valverde, yet grapples with Thibaut Courtois sidelined in goal, Rodrygo out indefinitely with a knee issue, Ferland Mendy doubtful, and Jude Bellingham's fitness concerns potentially benching the midfielder. Both sides' prolific UCL campaigns—Bayern's 32 goals in 10 games, Real's 29 in 12—coupled with suspension risks for stars like Mbappé, Bellingham, and Upamecano keep the matchup fiercely competitive.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from late March, including Paraná Pesquisas and BTG Pactual/Nexus, show President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva holding a narrow first-round lead over Senator Flávio Bolsonaro at around 41% to 38%, while runoff simulations depict them tied at 46% or Flávio edging ahead by 1-3 points, reflecting trader consensus on Polymarket where Lula trades at 41.5% implied probability. Flávio's surge stems from consolidating the right-wing base as a proxy for his ineligible father, Jair Bolsonaro, amid widespread voter discontent with the political class and Lula's administration facing economic headwinds. The race remains tight due to Brazil's deep polarization; separation could arise from economic recovery data favoring the incumbent, scandals impacting either side, or further opposition consolidations ahead of the October 4 first round.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Madrid CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid and Bayern Munich head into their UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at the Bernabéu amid injury crises tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive tie. Madrid's Thibaut Courtois is ruled out for both legs with a thigh injury, forcing Andriy Lunin into goal, while Rodrygo remains sidelined long-term by an ACL tear and Éder Militão nears return from a muscle issue around April 1. Bayern faces a goalkeeper nightmare with Manuel Neuer (calf), Sven Ulreich (adductor), and Jonas Urbig (concussion) unavailable, plus Jamal Musiala out with a fibula injury. Madrid's home advantage and recent La Liga momentum counter Bayern's strong aggregate win over Atalanta, but vulnerabilities on both benches heighten upset potential.

title: Who will be confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next individual formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's January nomination of former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh as next Fed Chair, formalized in March and backed by wide acclaim for his 2008 crisis stewardship, has solidified trader consensus at over 96% implied probability ahead of Jerome Powell's May 15 term end. The Senate Banking Committee's scheduling of Warsh's confirmation hearing for April 16 signals advancing momentum despite prior hurdles like Sen. Thom Tillis's holdout over a DOJ probe into Powell, recently complicated by quashed subpoenas. Realistic challenges include GOP defections in committee votes, hearing testimony missteps, or probe escalation derailing floor approval, potentially extending Powell's tenure as acting chair.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal tops trader consensus at 25.5% implied probability for UEFA Champions League glory, buoyed by a dominant 3-1 aggregate round-of-16 win over Bayer Leverkusen and a favorable quarterfinal path starting with an away first leg at Sporting CP on April 7. Bayern Munich trails closely at 22.5% after thrashing Atalanta 10-2 aggregate, yet faces a blockbuster Real Madrid tie amid Madrid's recent LaLiga slip and Kylian Mbappé's return from knee injury, though Thibaut Courtois and Rodrygo remain sidelined. Barcelona (17.5%) and PSG (15.5%) impressed with 8-3 and 8-2 aggregates respectively, but confront derby intensity against Atlético Madrid and high-stakes Liverpool clash, fostering a tightly contested knockout phase where no clear favorite dominates paths to the Budapest final.

title: Counter-Strike: B8 vs Inner Circle Esports (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 4 match between B8 and Inner Circle Esports in the PGL Bucharest Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 7 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "B8" if B8 win the match against Inner Circle Esports.

This market will resolve to "Inner Circle Esports" if Inner Circle Esports win the match against B8.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: ICE (-1.5) vs B8 (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 4 match between B8 and Inner Circle Esports in the PGL Bucharest Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 7 at 5:05AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Inner Circle Esports" if Inner Circle Esports wins 2 or more maps than B8 in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "B8". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the PGL Bucharest 2026 Swiss group stage round 4 elimination match, B8 (#18) faces Inner Circle (#38) in a high-stakes BO3, with the loser out and top 8 advancing to playoffs. Both enter at 1-2 records: B8 opened with a 2-0 win over Wildcard but dropped 1-2 decisions to Astralis and 3DMAX in the last 48 hours, exposing inconsistencies on Mirage and Inferno. Inner Circle stumbled early with 1-2 losses to FUT and Legacy before stunning FaZe 2-0 on Nuke and Mirage yesterday, fueled by recent signee headtr1ck (ex-B8). B8 holds higher recent form from winning Parken Challenger, while ICE's momentum and familiarity via headtr1ck add upset potential on the map pool of Dust2 (ICE pick), Mirage (B8 pick), and Nuke decider.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 3 - April 10, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 3 12:00 PM ET to April 10, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Elon Musk tweet count market for April 3–10 reflects his volatile posting habits, with implied probabilities tightly clustered around 240–299 posts (65.5% combined across top three bins) based on a recent daily average of 38 tweets from late March through early April 7. This pace—driven by steady engagement on X with politics, AI innovations like Grok Imagine, and cultural commentary—projects to roughly 266 tweets over the 7-day window, but wild swings (e.g., 74 on April 2–3 versus 8 on April 1–2) keep 260–279 (25.5%), 240–259 (21.5%), and 280–299 (18.5%) neck-and-neck. No confirmed catalysts like Tesla earnings or SpaceX launches loom in the final days, amplifying uncertainty in this high-volume celebrity social media tracker as traders weigh historical spikes against quieter periods.

title: LoL: T1 Academy vs Nongshim Esports Academy (BO3) - LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between T1 Academy and Nongshim Esports Academy in the LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 7 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "T1 Academy" if T1 Academy win the match against Nongshim Esports Academy.

This market will resolve to "Nongshim Esports Academy" if Nongshim Esports Academy win the match against T1 Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both T1 Academy and Nongshim Esports Academy each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both T1 Academy and Nongshim Esports Academy each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: T1 Esports Academy and Nongshim Esports Academy, both undefeated at 2-0 with 4-1 map differentials, collide in a pivotal Week 2 BO3 under Hard Fearless Draft rules in LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2 double round-robin. T1 carries momentum from a clean 2-0 sweep over Hanwha Life Esports Challengers on April 3 and a gritty 2-1 win against Kiwoom DRX Challengers on March 31, showcasing strong macro and carry potential from midlaner Guti and ADC Cypher. Nongshim impressed with a 2-0 domination of BNK FEARX Youth on April 2 following a 2-1 thriller over HANJIN BRION Challengers, led by jungler Mihawk's playmaking. Nongshim holds recent edge, claiming LCK CL Kickoff title via 3-1 finals win over T1 in early March; stable rosters and current meta favor aggressive early-game styles for both in this top-table battle.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by March 1st?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump stated on April 6 that U.S. military operations against Iran—launched February 28 with joint strikes alongside Israel to degrade missile capabilities and address Strait of Hormuz closure—have core strategic objectives nearing completion, following a successful rescue of downed airmen and over 7,000 targets hit. However, he issued fresh ultimatums threatening strikes on all Iranian power plants and bridges unless Tehran reopens the strait by April 8, signaling no formal end announcement amid ongoing airstrikes and Pentagon concerns over depleting high-value targets. Traders monitor this deadline for potential de-escalation talks or ground troop deployments, with Iran's retaliatory missile attacks on Israel and Gulf states adding uncertainty to any cessation declaration.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Keiko Fujimori as the slight frontrunner at 27% implied probability to win Peru's presidency, including any June runoff after the April 12 first round, with Carlos Álvarez surging to 24% and Rafael López Aliaga at 18.5%, reflecting a highly fragmented race among over 35 candidates. Recent Ipsos, Datum, and CPI polls from early April show Fujimori leading at 16-19% after steady gains from televised debates, Álvarez rising to 10-13% via media exposure, and López Aliaga slipping below 10%, amid 20-30% undecided voters disillusioned by political instability, crime surges, and protests. The tight contest persists due to undecided blocs, regional divides in Lima and the north, and no candidate nearing the 50% first-round threshold; late endorsements, turnout in battlegrounds like Cusco, or scandals could propel a clear runoff pair.
